perturbation.original_text: If Producer determines that the Submission ca nnot be further developed into a Project, within <….> months of the receipt of the Subm ission, Producer shall within five (5) business days after such determination return any and all Confidential Informati on to Writer, along with all copies or derivatives thereof and all writings generated by Producer in c onnection with Producer’s evaluation of the Submission or the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If Producer determines that the Submission ca nnot be further developed into a Project, Producer shall return any and all Confidential Informati on to Writer.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the *within <....> months* clause and the five (5) business days constraint, this introduces uncertainty about the timeline for returning confidential information if the producer decides not to develop the submission. This directly contradicts an expectation of timely return outlined in the original clause and creates uncertainty for when the writer could take the work to another producer.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Producer determines that the Submission ca nnot be further developed into a Project, Producer shall return any and all Confidential Informati on to Writer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'within <....> months of receipt' and 'five (5) business days after such determination' clauses, eliminating the timeframe for the producer to return confidential information. The contradiction explanation states that this introduces uncertainty, which directly contradicts an expectation of timely return. Although the changed and contradicted text are worded the same, the *changed text* does in fact create a contradiction to the *original* text, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. Therefore, the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' leads to a YES classification.
